# Joe Rogan Experience: #2223 with Elon Musk (Transcript)

* November 5, 2024 4:51 am December 19, 2025 2:27 am
* by [Pangambam S](https://singjupost.com/author/singju/ "Posts by Pangambam S")
* [Podcasts](https://singjupost.com/podcasts/)

* [Share](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fsingjupost.com%2Ffull-transcript-elon-musk-on-joe-rogan-podcast%2F&t=Joe%20Rogan%20Experience%3A%20%232223%20with%20Elon%20Musk%20%28Transcript%29 "Share on Facebook")
* [Tweet](https://x.com/intent/tweet?text=Joe%20Rogan%20Experience%3A%20%232223%20with%20Elon%20Musk%20%28Transcript%29&url=https%3A%2F%2Fsingjupost.com%2Ffull-transcript-elon-musk-on-joe-rogan-podcast%2F&via=singjupost "Share on X")
* Pinterest
* Email

Read the full transcript of Elon Musk’s interview on Joe Rogan Experience Podcast #2223 which was live on November 4, 2024.

**Listen to the audio version here:**

ELON MUSK: But if you want to sort of see like a vision of the future, it’s like basically like the top 20, or even the top 100, is like totally dominated by China.

JOE ROGAN: Really?

ELON MUSK: Yeah, there’s like China and a little bit of Korea and Taiwan.

JOE ROGAN: So are you in the top 20 in the world?

ELON MUSK: Yeah.

JOE ROGAN: Wow, in Diablo.

ELON MUSK: Yeah, yeah.

JOE ROGAN: Do you want to tell everybody your handle?

ELON MUSK: No.

JOE ROGAN: Don’t tell them. Don’t tell them, it’s not worth it.

ELON MUSK: Well, they’ve actually listed me with my actual name in their list.

JOE ROGAN: Oh, did they really?

ELON MUSK: Yeah.

JOE ROGAN: Oh, interesting.

ELON MUSK: But yeah, there’s only two Americans in the top 20. The rest, almost everyone is from Asia otherwise.

## Benefits of Video Games

JOE ROGAN: We were talking about something that I think is a really good, because people always think that video games are frivolous, but what you were saying I think that’s really important is it’s so difficult that it requires you to only think about that, and it can like relieve stress.

ELON MUSK: Yeah.

JOE ROGAN: It can take out the rest of the world because it’s so hard. You can only think about that.

ELON MUSK: I mean, if I play a video game on extreme difficulty, then I have to concentrate fully on the game, and it has a calming effect. It sort of chills down. I mean, you mentioned I think many people, like if you play martial arts or you play pool, like something that forces you, it’s like I think anything that forces you to concentrate fully actually has a calming effect. I find it just sort of like kind of a restoring effect. Like it’s good.

JOE ROGAN: Jiu-jitsu is like that. Archery is like that as well. When you’re shooting a bow, you have to, there’s so many moving things, and you’re trying, you have to think only of it, and it cleans the mind.

JOE ROGAN: **I was reading a study about surgeons where they found that surgeons who regularly play video games make less errors.**

ELON MUSK: Well, I mean, video games require manual dexterity.

JOE ROGAN: So it makes sense.

ELON MUSK: Actually, if somebody was like every good video games, I’d say like their surgical skills can be very good because in order to be good at video games, any kind of fast reaction video games.

JOE ROGAN: Look at this, 32% fewer errors, 24% faster, and scored 26% better overall than their non-player colleagues.

ELON MUSK: Oh, I believe that for sure.

JOE ROGAN: That’s incredible. Like that, you should be required in medical school to play video games. Don’t you think?

ELON MUSK: If somebody’s like a top ranked video game player, and they say they’re a surgeon, I’d be like plus one, plus two type of thing.

JOE ROGAN: Oh, top ranked for sure. This isn’t even top ranked. This is just people who play.

ELON MUSK: Well, your manual dexterity has to be extremely high.

JOE ROGAN: Yes.

ELON MUSK: You’re looking at things on the screen. You’re reacting, and you’ve got like 10 milliseconds to react.

JOE ROGAN: Yes.

ELON MUSK: And so if somebody’s got incredible reaction times and manual dexterity, they’re obviously going to be a good surgeon.

JOE ROGAN: Imagine if there was a course that you could take. That course would promote you would be 26% better.

ELON MUSK: Yeah.

JOE ROGAN: Everyone would have to take that course. Why would you want a surgeon that’s less prepared? You would say, “Hey, Bob, did you take this course? You didn’t take this course. Don’t you understand this course makes you 26% better?”

ELON MUSK: Sure. You would have to take it.

JOE ROGAN: Everyone should have to play video games if you want to be a surgeon.

ELON MUSK: Well, I think it certainly would be a very good test to see if somebody can’t play video games. Well, that means you’ve got to move both hands simultaneously. You’ve got to react to something very fast on the screen. And if your keystrokes or your mouse clicks or whatever are wrong, then you lose the game.

JOE ROGAN: Right.

ELON MUSK: So if somebody has a good rank in video games, I would say that necessarily their manual dexterity must be extremely good.

JOE ROGAN: Well, it’s so hard.

ELON MUSK: Their fine motor skills have to be excellent.

JOE ROGAN: **If you think about StarCraft or any game like Quake, any game where a lot of people are playing, to rise to the top, you have to be exceptional, period, as a human being.** There has to be something exceptional about you.

ELON MUSK: Yeah.

## Joe Rogan’s Quake Addiction

ELON MUSK: Actually, if I’m interested in Quake, way back in the day, I was one of the world’s best Quake players.

JOE ROGAN: I know. We talked about this.

ELON MUSK: Yeah. I loved Quake. My final semester in college, I probably put more time into Quake than all my college classes.

JOE ROGAN: When I was on news radio, all of the writers were super nerds. They were very, very fun guys. And they had a LAN set up at the studio where they all played Quake. I had never played video games. And I would go in with the writers and just kind of hang out with them. We’d get silly. And then we would all start playing video games and playing Quake against each other. And I got addicted, like hardcore. Like hardcore. I got a T1 line installed in my house.

ELON MUSK: Yeah.

JOE ROGAN: I went hardcore.

ELON MUSK: Yeah. Exactly. Checking how many milliseconds of latency.

JOE ROGAN: Oh, yeah. I was fully addicted. I was making my own computers. I was going to Fry’s Hardware and buying motherboards and putting everything together.

It was too much of a time suck, though. I’m an obsessive person. I can’t get involved. I can’t play golf.

ELON MUSK: No. Golf is too slow for me. I mean, I know a lot of people find golf good. I mean, I guess if you think of it like it’s – I guess if you’re saying you’re going to walk outdoors with friends. And you occasionally hit a ball and it’s an outdoor walk, then that’s cool. And this requires concentration when you’re hitting the ball, but it’s too slow for me.

JOE ROGAN: Nothing compares to video games in terms of like the amount of feedback you get.

ELON MUSK: Yeah.

JOE ROGAN: Like the sensory overload you get when you’re looking at a large, high-resolution screen. You have a fast computer. You have headphones on. You’re hearing sounds from here and sounds behind you and rockets are flying by you. There’s nothing like that. But I think golf still is like – Jamie will tell you. Jamie is an addict. He’s a golf nut. It’s super addictive and it takes like eight hours a day.

ELON MUSK: Yes. Once you get into golf, I think I guess any sport, it gets super addictive. But for me, the intensity of video games is heartbeat.

## Video Games as Mental Calibration

JOE ROGAN: Yes. And people dismiss it because they think it’s just a waste of time. But we’re showing like real-world benefits of people playing video games.

ELON MUSK: Yeah. If you want to be a drone operator, it’s the only game in town.

JOE ROGAN: Yeah. Absolutely. I mean, that’s really good at video games.

ELON MUSK: Yeah. For sure. In fact, I can actually tell like what my mental acuity is if I just play – if I play a very hard video game. So if I’m trying to sort of get like an extremely good clear time in Diablo or something like that or a first-person shooter, whatever the case may be, like if – I can tell that I’m tired or my brain is not working as well as it should. It’s like a mental calibration. You tell immediately like what is – how good is your mental state?

JOE ROGAN: Right. Right.

ELON MUSK: And so like if you’re trying to play really well, like if you play late at night and you’re tired, you just play badly. And you can say, okay, you may think that your brain is working well, but it isn’t.

JOE ROGAN: Yeah.

ELON MUSK: You play the video game and you’re like, you suck. So, okay.

JOE ROGAN: Yeah, you’re putting it under stress. Yeah. You’re really stress testing it.

ELON MUSK: You stress test it and – Because like sometimes like, oh, I think I’m fine. But then play a game like, okay, I’m not – I’m like 10% below what I should be.

JOE ROGAN: That’s how I feel about workouts for sure. Like that’s how I knew I had COVID or I knew – everyone in my family had COVID. And felt weaker. And so I was trying to not get COVID. And so I was working out. I was like, something’s up. Like I felt fine normally, but then during exercise I was like, okay, I can tell there’s something wrong here. So let’s back off, relax.

ELON MUSK: Yeah. Yeah.

JOE ROGAN: It’s like people who don’t stress test their mind, they think they’re operating on the same level all the time. Like sometimes I come in here and I can’t form a fucking sentence and I don’t know what it is. It’s like, what is going on?

ELON MUSK: Yeah. So it’s just like – Maybe it’s like my sleep wasn’t that good or something like that.

JOE ROGAN: Yeah. Or I’m too busy and it’s just – it’s not – the words aren’t coming out. Like I know how to talk. Yeah. I talk professionally and I can’t fucking talk. It’s like –

ELON MUSK: I mean sleep is massive. I mean like –

JOE ROGAN: Huge.

ELON MUSK: Yeah. **So I can tell immediately, did I get a good night’s sleep or not? If I just play like a video game for like five minutes.** I’m like, okay, my sleep wasn’t that good because it’s my – you know. And sometimes it’ll – your brain will recover through the day and it’s like, okay, like an hour or two after waking up, it’s better. Because your brain does kind of recover from bad night’s sleep a little bit.

## Benefits of Creatine

JOE ROGAN: Do you know what really helps? Creatine apparently.

ELON MUSK: Does it?

JOE ROGAN: Yeah. **Creatine is actually a nootropic, believe it or not. There’s a lot of like benefits of creatine that are really weird.**

ELON MUSK: Are there any downsides?

JOE ROGAN: No. No. It’s a natural part of food. Yeah. Yeah. Especially women. For women apparently, especially post-menopausal women, it’s very beneficial. And there’s – but there’s a lot of like cognitive benefits. And one of the big ones that they found recently is performance when sleep-deprived.

ELON MUSK: Oh, okay.

JOE ROGAN: Mental performance when sleep-deprived increases pretty measurably when you supplement with creatine.

ELON MUSK: Is creatine naturally occurring in like steak or –

JOE ROGAN: Yeah. It’s like naturally occurring in meat, I think. I think that’s where it’s coming from. I think it’s primarily an animal-based thing.

ELON MUSK: But like I did switch to like steak and eggs for breakfast and I found that’s like a power-up.

JOE ROGAN: Oh, yeah. Yeah. Yeah. Well, we’re all overrun with carbohydrates. And you just – like carbohydrates make this big crash, the rise and the crash.

ELON MUSK: Yeah. Yeah. Totally.

JOE ROGAN: The rise and the crash. You stay flat if you eat like a primarily high-protein, high-fat diet. Your body runs off ketosis essentially.

ELON MUSK: Yeah. I mean I just have like steak and eggs, no bread or anything.

JOE ROGAN: Yeah. It’s great.

ELON MUSK: It’s great actually. It’s a power-up I’d say.

## Misconceptions About Animal Agriculture and Global Warming

JOE ROGAN: People dismiss this whole carnivore diet thing because in our heads there’s a lot of propaganda around this that put this thing out there that animal agriculture is the number one contributor to global warming.

ELON MUSK: Yeah. It’s rubbish.

JOE ROGAN: Bullshit.

ELON MUSK: It’s not true. Bullshit. It’s hot bullshit. It doesn’t matter.

JOE ROGAN: Not only is it hot bullshit. **It’s irrelevant. But the real problem is factory farming.** Regenerative farming is carbon neutral if it doesn’t sequester carbon.

ELON MUSK: The animals are not going to make any difference to global warming. Like none.

JOE ROGAN: No. It’s bullshit.

ELON MUSK: Zero. It’s zero. It’s nothing.

JOE ROGAN: Do you see people that have a vested interest in like plant-based meat products and things along those lines, green energy?

ELON MUSK: I think it’s part of it. You know, you’re generally going to get people pushing to avoid meat. Like some people just, you know, maybe they’ve got a financial interest, maybe they’re just like vegetarians or vegans or whatever. Ideological reasons. But it’s not going to make any difference to global warming or, you know, the CO2 concentration in the atmosphere, really, if people eat pure steaks. It doesn’t matter. It’s irrelevant. Irrelevant. I want to just be super clear about that. It will not matter. You will not even be able to measure it. Okay? That’s how irrelevant it is.

JOE ROGAN: Isn’t it funny that that’s heretic-speaking? Unmeasurable irrelevant. Like that’s crazy talk now. Nowadays, it’s like you have to say that we have to eat less meat. That meat is bad.

ELON MUSK: No. You can totally eat as much meat as you want. It’s not going to make a difference.

JOE ROGAN: Sing it. Sing it. Tell the world.

ELON MUSK: Yeah. Yeah. Absolutely. And if somebody says it does make a difference, I’m like, how will you measure it? And if you can’t even measure it, then it’s bullshit.

JOE ROGAN: Yeah. Yeah. Well, there’s so much bullshit out there. First of all, thank you so much for buying Twitter.

ELON MUSK: You’re welcome. Thank you so much.

JOE ROGAN: **I’m not exaggerating when I think you changed the course of history. I really do. I really think you made a fork in the road. We were headed down a path of censorship and of control of narratives that is unprecedented.** Forget about what they were able to do back when they had newspapers and the media under control. What they were doing with social media by suppressing information, and when you had a combined government effort, like what they were doing with the laptop story. We have 51 former intelligence agents saying that this is Russian disinformation. Take it off offline.

ELON MUSK: Yeah.

JOE ROGAN: And Twitter complied. If you didn’t buy that, we wouldn’t have known that. We had no idea.

ELON MUSK: Exactly. No, it’s… I mean, the reason I bought it was because I’m pretty attuned, since I was like the most interacted with a user on Twitter before the acquisition. So before the acquisition, I had more interactions than then. Like, there’s some accounts, like Obama and whatever, had higher follow accounts, but I had the most number of interactions of any account in the system. So I was very attuned to like, if they start changing, if they change this system, I can tell immediately. And I’m like, something weird is going on here. You know?

JOE ROGAN: Yeah.

ELON MUSK: There’s like, I just got increasingly uneasy. And obviously, when they de-platformed a sitting president, you know, de-platformed Trump, that was just insane, you know? And the things he was posting, like he was posting things that, like he was posting good things. He was saying like, “Hey, do not riot, don’t do any destruction of property, you know, please stay calm.” Like, that’s the kind of stuff he was posting.

JOE ROGAN: Yeah.

ELON MUSK: And you’re like, what’s wrong with that? And then some people said like, “Oh, that’s like some sort of dog whistle. He means the opposite.” I’m like, okay, so we’ll give you Trump’s account. Now you post what you think you should post, because you can post nothing. You can ask people to calm down. Like what? It was insane. It doesn’t make any sense.

JOE ROGAN: Well, it’s completely illogical when you say it’s dog whistling to tell his followers to not be violent. That’s crazy. Crazy. That’s crazy.

ELON MUSK: Crazy.

JOE ROGAN: Don’t you think they’ll listen to him? Isn’t that the whole point? They listened to him and created violence in the first place? That’s what you think.

ELON MUSK: Yes, exactly.

JOE ROGAN: That’s what you’re accusing him of. Right. And then there’s the fact that we know that there was agents in the crowd that were agent provocateurs that were encouraging people to do illegal shit.

ELON MUSK: Yes.

JOE ROGAN: We know that for a fact. That was always the big Alex Jones type tinfoil hat conspiracy theory, because Alex proposed that back at the World Trade Organization protests. I believe we were in Seattle in the 90s. And they sent in agent provocateurs, started smashing things, lighting things on fire. Now all of a sudden the peaceful protest is no longer peaceful. They move in the cops. They shut everything down. They had it set up where it was a no protest zone, where you couldn’t even have a pin that had the WTO with a red line through it. They wouldn’t let you go in through to go to work. So you couldn’t protest. You couldn’t exercise your First Amendment rights. You couldn’t even like have a peaceful protest, a fucking sticker on your car. You couldn’t have that.

ELON MUSK: It’s crazy. It’s crazy. So, no, I think we’re very much a fork in the road in destiny. And so, I mean, the reason I did the Twitter acquisition was like, man, if I don’t do this, I think we’re screwed. That’s the issue.

JOE ROGAN: Well, if you didn’t do it, no one else was going to do it. Because it wasn’t a financial winner. It was kind of a crazy move.

ELON MUSK: It’s a crazy move. I mean, the thing was way overpriced. And you know, like long term, I think we can ultimately make it a win for investors. But boy, this is a hard way to make a living.

JOE ROGAN: Well, there’s also a concerted effort to suppress it. There’s a concerted effort with the advertisers.

ELON MUSK: We had and still have a massive advertiser boycott that was organized by a bunch of left wing NGOs. Like, you know, and I should have brought my, I have a hat, “Make All Well Fiction Again”.

JOE ROGAN: I’ve seen that.

ELON MUSK: Yeah. I was going to wear my, I should have brought my “Make All Well Fiction Hat Again”. But yeah, I mean, it’s just totally, totally nuts.

JOE ROGAN: So if you didn’t do it, no one would have. And here’s the hilarious narrative that I keep hearing from idiots. “Elon’s a bad businessman. Twitter is worth, you know, 400% less than when he bought it.” No, it wasn’t worth that in the first place.

ELON MUSK: It wasn’t worth that in the first place.

JOE ROGAN: It wasn’t worth $44 billion, you fucking morons. Like wrong. And also, you’re not taking into account the advertiser boycott. That’s total bullshit.

ELON MUSK: Yeah, exactly. So there are these organizations, like you can tell there’s like, they’re like, like, when they have an Orwellian name. So like, like the Center for Countering Digital Hate, right, is a total scam organization, you know, because they’re like the Ministry of Truth thing in Orwell. You know, they’re like, they’re a censorship organization.

And they organized and they pushed the advertisers to boycott. So we still have like some of the boycott is, is starting to lift. And I think if Trump wins, we’ll see, you know, probably a lot, most, most of the boycott lift. But if Kamala wins, we’ll see that boycott gets stronger. And they’ll, they’ll friggin shut down. There’s no way that, that, that sort of Kamala, Kamala public regime would allow X to exist.

JOE ROGAN: You really think that they’ll be able to shut it down, though? Is there a pathway to that?

ELON MUSK: Yes.

JOE ROGAN: What would they do?

ELON MUSK: Well, I mean, they can just, they can stick the DOJ on, you know, and say like, you know, they’ve had this whole thing about like hate speech, misinformation, whatever, except that the other one is pushing the misinformation, but that doesn’t stop them from filing massive, you know, lawsuits and using the DOJ. I mean, like the DOJ is, you know, attacking SpaceX, for example, for not hiring asylum speakers, even though it is legal for SpaceX to hire anyone who’s not a permanent resident of the U. S. So we’re damned if we do and damned if we don’t. It doesn’t exist. Just an example of what DOJ can do.

JOE ROGAN: So it’s illegal to hire someone who’s not an American citizen.

ELON MUSK: SpaceX is considered an advanced weapons technology. So it’s covered by international traffic and arms regulations because we make rocket technology that can be used against the United States. It’s like if North Korea or Iran got SpaceX rocket technology, they could use that to launch nukes at America.

JOE ROGAN: Right. That would be bad.

ELON MUSK: Yeah. That’d be really bad. That’d be really bad. So since we are in like the most extreme category of weapons technology at SpaceX, under U. S. ITAR law, it is illegal for us to hire anyone who’s not a permanent resident because the presumption is that if they’re not a permanent resident, they’re going to return to their home country and take the rocket technology with them.

So it’s illegal for us to hire anyone who either has a green card or be a citizen. They just have to be a permanent resident of the United States. Then there’s another law that says if you discriminate against asylum seekers, you’re also breaking the law. So the DOJ can only do a small number of big lawsuits every year. **There’s a giant lawsuit against SpaceX saying that SpaceX discriminated against asylum seekers.** And we’re like, but it’s illegal for us to hire anyone who’s not a permanent resident. So we’re in this like, this is what I mean, it’s like the Orwell situation is getting insane. Like you’re damned if you do and you’re damned if you don’t. So you’re damned.

JOE ROGAN: Do you imagine history looking back at when you watch the robot arms catch the rocket and you realize like, this is like one of the greatest accomplishments in the history of aerospace. Like it is one of the most wildest accomplishments. When you watch that thing come and you see all those people cheering and it catches it perfectly like holy shit. Imagine how history is going to look back at the DOJ going after that company. How insane it is.

ELON MUSK: It’s like a big lawsuit with an army of lawyers. Like this was not like some minor thing.

JOE ROGAN: But it doesn’t even make any sense logically. How can it even get brought to court if it’s illegal?

ELON MUSK: Exactly. So that’s what I mean. Like basically if the government wants to go after you, they’ll just find a reason. It’s like that famous quote from Beria, you know, like Stalin’s chief torturer, the head of Stalin’s secret police and he’s like chief torture, truly evil human being like this guy Beria. One of his famous quotes was, “Show me the man and I’ll show you the crime.” They just, they just, they like, they decide that you’re the target and then they figure out the crime afterwards. That’s the issue. They decided SpaceX was the target. They just figured out the crime afterwards.

JOE ROGAN: Which is so crazy because that’s exactly what they’re saying Trump is going to do if he gets into office.

ELON MUSK: They’re doing all the things that they accused Trump of doing.

JOE ROGAN: Yeah. Openly.

ELON MUSK: Openly.

JOE ROGAN: Yeah.

ELON MUSK: The sheer number of hoaxes that the democratic party is pushing over and over again, they, and it’s like, look, I just sound like politicians are going to, you know, exaggerate. They’re going to misspeak and they’ll tell occasional, you know, untruths, whatever. That’s how it is in politics. But when you have deliberate, concerted, repeated pushing of hoaxes, you’re like, wait a second. Like, come on man. This is too, this is too far. And you’re supposed to be the good guys. And you claim to be the good guys.

ELON MUSK: I’m like, exactly.

JOE ROGAN: You’re supposed to be the progressives.

ELON MUSK: Yes. The Dems are like, oh, we’re the good guys. We’re the honest people. No, no. Hang on. You can’t claim to be the good guys. You can’t claim to be the honest people. If you’re deliberately pushing hoaxes that have been debunked thoroughly, like even Snopes, which is a liberal thing because it’s bogus, like the fine people hoax.

JOE ROGAN: Obama just said that on stage. Obama just said that. I was like, what the flying fuck? He doesn’t give a fuck. He doesn’t give a fuck. They’re just, they’re just flowing forward. That’s a flat out goddamn fucking lie. Flat out lie. Flat out fucking lie. How about the other one where Kamala’s campaign used what Trump was saying about protecting women from illegal immigrants?

JOE ROGAN: You remember that?

ELON MUSK: Yeah.

JOE ROGAN: What he was saying, “If the women like it or not, I’m going to do it.” When he was saying that, they were trying to say that he was taking away women’s right to choose whether women like it or not. Like that’s not what he was saying.

ELON MUSK: Absolutely.

JOE ROGAN: He was literally talking about protecting them from dangerous people that are sneaking in through the border.

JOE ROGAN: Yes, exactly. They’ll take like, like not even a full sentence, like a half a sentence from this, and then they’ll push it on every ad, every, you know, every speaking event. And it gets repeated on the news.

ELON MUSK: Yes. This is what’s crazy.

JOE ROGAN: They’ll talk about it on these new shows, quote new shows.

ELON MUSK: Yeah, exactly. I mean, I mean, a recent one that came up, which had a lot of people, because a lot of people reached out to me, it was like, they’re like, “Oh, Trump says he wants to execute Liz Cheney.” And I’m like, that is utter bullshit. It’s not what he said at all. It’s not what he said at all. All he said was like, what he’s saying is that, look, if Liz Cheney actually had to fight at the front lines, she’d think twice about going to war.

JOE ROGAN: Exactly.

ELON MUSK: Like, it’s easy to, it’s easy to go to war. It’s easy to be a warmonger if you don’t have to, you know, risk dying at the front lines. Like if other, like basically gets fucked up if people are having like fancy dinners in Washington, D.C. while people are being slaughtered in trenches, you know, it’s like you’re not feeling the pain.

ELON MUSK: You’re not taking the risk. It’s someone else dying. That’s like, that’s, that’s cruel and lacking in empathy. And all Trump was saying was that it’s like Liz Cheney would be much, Liz Cheney would be much less of a warmonger because she’s a huge warmonger, just like her dad, if she actually had to go to the front lines and fight herself.

JOE ROGAN: And meanwhile, they’re saying that he should, he’s saying she should be shot.

ELON MUSK: Yes.

JOE ROGAN: Which is a total lie.

ELON MUSK: But I had like tons of people calling me this weekend saying, “Oh, Trump says he’s going to put Liz Cheney in a firing squad.” I’m like, that is an outrageous lie. And legacy media ran with that lie big time.

JOE ROGAN: Yeah, it’s crazy. It’s just wild to see. And if it wasn’t for Twitter or X now, I don’t think we would know about all this stuff. I think it would be very difficult for you. I think YouTube throttled. They did something weird. They won’t say what they did, but they did something weird with the Trump interview that I did.
